memories.content: User prefers to batch-process emails twice a day (9am and 3pm) rather than checking continuously. Said constant notifications were a major source of anxiety and switching to batch processing cut email time by 40%.

User tends to overcommit on weekends — packing Saturday and Sunday with activities then feeling exhausted Monday. Learned this after three consecutive Mondays where user reported burnout. Now recommending maximum 2 planned activities per weekend day with buffer time built in.

User completed their first 6-mile run (week 7 of half marathon training). Felt strong and maintained a 10:15 pace. This was a confidence boost after struggling with the 5-miler the week before. Celebrated with a smoothie from the place on Hawthorne.

User's mother (Linda) lives in Eugene, about 2 hours south. They talk on the phone every Sunday evening. User mentioned feeling guilty about not visiting more often. Recommended scheduling one visit per month — user agreed and seemed relieved to have it as a calendar item rather than a worry.

User does best with habit stacking — linking new habits to existing ones. Successfully built a meditation habit by attaching it to morning coffee. Applying same strategy: vitamins after brushing teeth, stretching after brushing teeth at night, reading before sleep instead of phone scrolling.
